mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate applied to future earnings and raises equity duration value, so negative 3-month changes in FEDFUNDS predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if declining FEDFUNDS observations do not deliver higher mean 20-day S&P 500 returns than rising FEDFUNDS observations, or if the walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 for 24 months.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 8 percent, household income risk and earnings downgrades dominate valuation support, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE-above-8 observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60 percent of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.5 percent while UNRATE is above 8 percent, policy is maximally accommodative during labor slack, so discount-rate compression dominates weak earnings and predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is 0.17 and UNRATE is 9.0 in the snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-FEDFUNDS high-UNRATE state has lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than all other states in two consecutive walk-forward evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in RSAFS indicates strengthening nominal consumer demand and higher near-term revenue expectations, so rising 3-month RSAFS changes predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum fails to produce higher mean 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative RSAFS momentum at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in INDPRO signals falling capacity use and weaker operating leverage, so a negative industrial production shock predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contraction episodes have nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if their hit rate for negative returns is below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses below 600 thousand units, residential investment and housing-linked consumption are impaired, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and predicting negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-600 triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60 percent of walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5 percent signals rising inflation risk without offsetting policy tightening, compressing real purchasing power and predicting negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rate crosses above its 12-month rate and FEDFUNDS < 0.5
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL is 220.186 and FEDFUNDS is 0.17 in the snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-acceleration and low-FEDFUNDS condition has positive average 5-day returns or a negative-return hit rate below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises while UNRATE remains elevated, the economy is transitioning from labor slack to rehiring, improving earnings expectations and risk appetite, so the signal predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value is 130265.0 and UNRATE is 9.0
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-up high-UNRATE state fails to outperform the unconditional 60-day S&P 500 return by at least 1 percentage point on average.
